paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In late 1946 or early 1947, three Bedouin teenagers were tending their goats and sheep near the ancient settlement of Qumran, located on the northwest shore of the Dead Sea in what is now known as the West Bank. One of these young shepherds tossed a rock into an opening on the side of a cliff and was surprised to hear a shattering sound. He and his companions later entered the cave and stumbled across a collection of large clay jars, seven of which contained scrolls with writing on them. The teenagers took the seven scrolls to a nearby town where they were sold for a small sum to a local antiquities dealer. Word of the find spread, and Bedouins and archaeologists eventually unearthed tens of thousands of additional scroll fragments from 10 nearby caves; together they make up between 800 and 900 manuscripts. It soon became clear that this was one of the greatest archaeological discoveries ever made.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The origin of the Dead Sea Scrolls, which were written around 2,000 years ago between 150 BCE and 70 CE, is still the subject of scholarly debate even today. According to the prevailing theory, they are the work of a population that inhabited the area until Roman troops destroyed the settlement around 70 CE. The area was known as Judea at that time, and the people are thought to have belonged to a group called the Essenes, a devout Jewish sect.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The majority of the texts on the Dead Sea Scrolls are in Hebrew, with some fragments written in an ancient version of its alphabet thought to have fallen out of use in the fifth century BCE. But there are other languages as well. Some scrolls are in Aramaic, the language spoken by many inhabitants of the region from the sixth century BCE to the siege of Jerusalem in 70 CE. In addition, several texts feature translations of the Hebrew Bible into Greek.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The Dead Sea Scrolls include fragments from every book of the Old Testament of the Bible except for the Book of Esther. The only entire book of the Hebrew Bible preserved among the manuscripts from Qumran is Isaiah; this copy, dated to the first century BCE, is considered the earliest biblical manuscript still in existence. Along with biblical texts, the scrolls include documents about sectarian regulations and religious writings that do not appear in the Old Testament.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The writing on the Dead Sea Scrolls is mostly in black or occasionally red ink, and the scrolls themselves are nearly all made of either parchment (animal skin) or an early form of paper called 'papyrus'. The only exception is the scroll numbered 3Q15, which was created out of a combination of copper and tin. Known as the Copper Scroll, this curious document features letters chiselled onto metal – perhaps, as some have theorized, to better withstand the passage of time. One of the most intriguing manuscripts from Qumran, this is a sort of ancient treasure map that lists dozens of gold and silver caches. Using an unconventional vocabulary and odd spelling, it describes 64 underground hiding places that supposedly contain riches buried for safekeeping. None of these hoards have been recovered, possibly because the Romans pillaged Judea during the first century CE. According to various hypotheses, the treasure belonged to local people, or was rescued from the Second Temple before its destruction or never existed to begin with.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Some of the Dead Sea Scrolls have been on interesting journeys. In 1948, a Syrian Orthodox archbishop known as Mar Samuel acquired four of the original seven scrolls from a Jerusalem shoemaker and part-time antiquity dealer, paying less than $100 for them. He then travelled to the United States and unsuccessfully offered them to a number of universities, including Yale. Finally, in 1954, he placed an advertisement in the business newspaper The Wall Street Journal – under the category 'Miscellaneous Items for Sale' – that read: 'Biblical Manuscripts dating back to at least 200 B.C. are for sale. This would be an ideal gift to an educational or religious institution by an individual or group.' Fortunately, Israeli archaeologist and statesman Yigael Yadin negotiated their purchase and brought the scrolls back to Jerusalem, where they remain to this day.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In 2017, researchers from the University of Haifa restored and deciphered one of the last untranslated scrolls. The university's Eshbal Ratson and Jonathan Ben-Dov spent one year reassembling the 60 fragments that make up the scroll. Deciphered from a band of coded text on parchment, the find provides insight into the community of people who wrote it and the 364-day calendar they would have used. The scroll names celebrations that indicate shifts in seasons and details two yearly religious events known from another Dead Sea Scroll. Only one more known scroll remains untranslated.

groups.title: The Dead Sea Scrolls

Questions 1–5

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect: Discovery Qumran, 1946/7 • three Bedouin shepherds in their teens were near an opening on side of cliff • heard a noise of breaking when one teenager threw a $201$ • teenagers went into the $202$ and found a number of containers made of $203$ The scrolls • date from between 150 BCE and 70 CE • thought to have been written by group of people known as the $204$ • written mainly in the $205$ language • most are on religious topics, written using ink on parchment or papyrus

Questions 6–13

questions.title: The Bedouin teenagers who found the scrolls were disappointed by how little money they received for them.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“disappointed”在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词，原文只提到青少年以“小额”卖出了卷轴，但没有提到他们是否对这个金额感到失望。因此，题干中的信息在原文中没有明确提到，答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The teenagers took the seven scrolls to a nearby town where they were sold for a small sum to a local antiquities dealer.

questions.title: There is agreement among academics about the origin of the Dead Sea Scrolls.

central_sentences.sentence: The origin of the Dead Sea Scrolls, which were written around 2,000 years ago between 150 BCE and 70 CE, is still the subject of scholarly debate even today.

central_sentences.sentence: The Dead Sea Scrolls include fragments from every book of the Old Testament of the Bible except for the Book of Esther. The only entire book of the Hebrew Bible preserved among the manuscripts from Qumran is Isaiah; this copy, dated to the first century BCE, is considered the earliest biblical manuscript still in existence.
central_sentences.translation: 死海古卷包含了旧约圣经每一卷书的片段，除了以斯帖记。在库姆兰发现的手稿中，只有以赛亚书是完整保存的；这份抄本可以追溯到公元前一世纪，被认为是现存最早的圣经手稿。

content.title: A second attempt at domesticating the tomato
content.summary: {'A': '通过基因编辑技术，巴西和中国的两个研究团队在不到三年的时间内重新驯化了番茄，并使其比现有的番茄更加营养丰富。这种快速驯化的方法有助于改善现有作物，同时也可以将成千上万种野生植物转化为有用和吸引人的食物，从而使世界的食物供应更加健康和抗病性更强。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了野生番茄在人类选择和繁殖中经历了基因突变，导致了更大的果实，但是每次从大群体中选取一个突变植株进行繁殖时，会丧失很多基因多样性，并且有时候有利的突变也会伴随着不利的特征。现在，生物学家通过比较现代植物与野生亲属的基因组，已经开始重新引入这些变化，同时保持甚至增强野生品种的有利特征。', 'C': '这段话主要讲了关于番茄基因编辑的内容，包括通过编辑FRUIT WEIGHT基因使果实大小增加三倍，通过编辑MULTIFLORA基因使每个花序中的番茄数量增加，并且成功增加了番茄中的番茄红素含量，提高了耐病性和耐盐性，以及增加了维生素C含量。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了纽约州Boyce Thompson研究所的Joyce Van Eck首次使用相同的方法来驯化地樱桃或金苹果（Physalis pruinosa）。这种水果看起来与密切相关的秘鲁金苹果（Physalis peruviana）相似。Van Eck的团队已经编辑了这些植物，增加了果实的大小，使它们的生长更加紧凑，并阻止了果实的掉落。Van Eck表示：“这有潜力成为一种商业作物。”但她补充说，进一步开展工作将会很昂贵，因为需要支付CRISPR技术的许可证费用并获得监管批准。', 'E': '通过这种方法，可以推动许多不常见的植物的使用，但新食物要成为农民和消费者喜爱的主要作物将很困难。同时，通过选择耐旱或耐热的野生植物，我们可以创造出即使在地球变暖的情况下也能茁壮成长的作物。此外，CRISPR技术的简化使得任何具备相关技能的人都可以进行这项工作。'}
content.notes: ["* mutations: changes in an organism's genetic structure that can be passed down to later generations"]

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text:
It took at least 3,000 years for humans to learn how to domesticate the wild tomato and cultivate it for food. Now two separate teams in Brazil and China have done it all over again in less than three years. And they have done it better in some ways, as the re-domesticated tomatoes are more nutritious than the ones we eat at present.
This approach relies on the revolutionary CRISPR genome editing technique, in which changes are deliberately made to the DNA of a living cell, allowing genetic material to be added, removed or altered. The technique could not only improve existing crops, but could also be used to turn thousands of wild plants into useful and appealing foods. In fact, a third team in the US has already begun to do this with a relative of the tomato called the groundcherry.
This fast-track domestication could help make the world's food supply healthier and far more resistant to diseases, such as the rust fungus devastating wheat crops.
'This could transform what we eat,' says Jorg Kudla at the University of Munster in Germany, a member of the Brazilian team. 'There are 50,000 edible plants in the world, but 90 percent of our energy comes from just 15 crops.'
'We can now mimic the known domestication course of major crops like rice, maize, sorghum or others,' says Caixia Gao of the Chinese Academy of Sciences in Beijing. 'Then we might try to domesticate plants that have never been domesticated.'

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text:
Wild tomatoes, which are native to the Andes region in South America, produce pea-sized fruits. Over many generations, peoples such as the Aztecs and Incas transformed the plant by selecting and breeding plants with mutations* in their genetic structure, which resulted in desirable traits such as larger fruit.
But every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding, much genetic diversity is lost. And sometimes the desirable mutations come with less desirable traits. For instance, the tomato strains grown for supermarkets have lost much of their flavour.
By comparing the genomes of modern plants to those of their wild relatives, biologists have been working out what genetic changes occurred as plants were domesticated. The teams in Brazil and China have now used this knowledge to reintroduce these changes from scratch while maintaining or even enhancing the desirable traits of wild strains.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
Kudla's team made six changes altogether. For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.
While the historical domestication of tomatoes reduced levels of the red pigment lycopene – thought to have potential health benefits – the team in Brazil managed to boost it instead. The wild tomato has twice as much lycopene as cultivated ones; the newly domesticated one has five times as much.
'They are quite tasty,' says Kudla. 'A little bit strong. And very aromatic.'
The team in China re-domesticated several strains of wild tomatoes with desirable traits lost in domesticated tomatoes. In this way they managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race, which can devastate yields. They also created another strain that is more salt tolerant – and has higher levels of vitamin C.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text:
Meanwhile, Joyce Van Eck at the Boyce Thompson Institute in New York state decided to use the same approach to domesticate the groundcherry or goldenberry (Physalis pruinosa) for the first time. This fruit looks similar to the closely related Cape gooseberry (Physalis peruviana).
Groundcherries are already sold to a limited extent in the US but they are hard to produce because the plant has a sprawling growth habit and the small fruits fall off the branches when ripe. Van Eck's team has edited the plants to increase fruit size, make their growth more compact and to stop fruits dropping. 'There's potential for this to be a commercial crop,' says Van Eck. But she adds that taking the work further would be expensive because of the need to pay for a licence for the CRISPR technology and get regulatory approval.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
This approach could boost the use of many obscure plants, says Jonathan Jones of the Sainsbury Lab in the UK. But it will be hard for new foods to grow so popular with farmers and consumers that they become new staple crops, he thinks.
The three teams already have their eye on other plants that could be 'catapulted into the mainstream', including foxtail, oat-grass and cowpea. By choosing wild plants that are drought or heat tolerant, says Gao, we could create crops that will thrive even as the planet warms.
But Kudla didn't want to reveal which species were in his team's sights, because CRISPR has made the process so easy. 'Anyone with the right skills could go to their lab and do this.'

groups.desc:
Questions 14–18

Reading Passage 2 has five sections, A–E.
Which section contains the following information?
Tick the correct letter, A–E, for questions 14-18.
NB You may use any letter more than once.

questions.title: a reference to a type of tomato that can resist a dangerous infection
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是段落C，因为段落C中明确提到了中国团队重新驯化了一种对细菌斑点病具有抗性的番茄品种，这与题目中的“a type of tomato that can resist a dangerous infection”相对应。题目中的“resist”对应原文中的“resistant to”，“infection”对应原文中的“disease”。

central_sentences.sentence: In this way they managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race, which can devastate yields.
central_sentences.translation: 通过这种方法，他们成功创造了一种能够抵抗细菌斑点病（一种常见的可能严重影响产量的疾病）的番茄品种。

target_sen.target_sen: In this way they managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race, which can devastate yields.
In this way they managed to create a strain resistant to a common disease called bacterial spot race, which can devastate yields.

questions.title: an explanation of how problems can arise from focusing only on a certain type of tomato plant.
questions.analysis: 段落B详细解释了专注于某种特定番茄可能带来的问题。每次从一个较大的种群中选择一个带有变异的单一植物进行繁殖时，都会失去大量的基因多样性。而且有时理想的变异会伴随着不理想的特性。这正是题干中所提到的问题。

central_sentences.sentence: But every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding, much genetic diversity is lost. And sometimes the desirable mutations come with less desirable traits.
central_sentences.translation: 但每次从更大的种群中选择一株带有突变的植物进行繁育时，大量的基因多样性就会丢失。有时理想的突变也会伴随着一些不太理想的特征。

target_sen.target_sen: But every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding, much genetic diversity is lost.
And sometimes the desirable mutations come with less desirable traits.

syn_ary.synonym: every time a single plant with a mutation is taken from a larger population for breeding

tomato plant

questions.title: a number of examples of plants that are not cultivated at present but could be useful as food sources
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "a number of examples of plants that are not cultivated at present but could be useful as food sources" 对应的是段落 E。段落 E 中提到的 "catapulted into the mainstream" 表示这些植物目前尚未广泛种植，但有潜力成为重要的食物来源。具体的例子包括 "foxtail, oat-grass and cowpea"，这与题干中的 "a number of examples" 对应。因此，段落 E 是正确的匹配。

central_sentences.sentence: The three teams already have their eye on other plants that could be 'catapulted into the mainstream', including foxtail, oat-grass and cowpea.
central_sentences.translation: 这三个团队已经将目光投向了其他可能被"推向主流"的植物，包括狐尾草、燕麦草和豇豆。

target_sen.target_sen: The three teams already have their eye on other plants that could be 'catapulted into the mainstream', including foxtail, oat-grass and cowpea.

By choosing wild plants that are drought or heat tolerant, says Gao, we could create crops that will thrive even as the planet warms.

questions.title: a comparison between the early domestication of the tomato and more recent research
questions.analysis: 题干中的“comparison between the early domestication of the tomato and more recent research”要求找到早期驯化番茄与最近研究的比较。段落A中明确提到“3,000 years”和“less than three years”，这两个时间对比直接展示了早期驯化与最近研究的区别。因此，段落A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: It took at least 3,000 years for humans to learn how to domesticate the wild tomato and cultivate it for food. Now two separate teams in Brazil and China have done it all over again in less than three years.
central_sentences.translation: 人类花了至少3000年的时间学会如何驯化野生番茄并将其培育成食物。而现在，巴西和中国的两个独立团队在不到三年的时间内就重新完成了这个过程。

questions.title: a personal reaction to the flavour of a tomato that has been genetically edited
questions.analysis: 这道题的题干信息是“a personal reaction to the flavour of a tomato that has been genetically edited”，意思是对经过基因编辑的番茄味道的个人反应。段落C中，Kudla对番茄的味道进行了评价，使用了“tasty”、“strong”和“aromatic”这些词汇来描述番茄的味道，并且明确指出这是Kudla的个人反应（says Kudla）。因此，段落C是最符合题干信息的段落。

central_sentences.sentence: 'They are quite tasty,' says Kudla. 'A little bit strong. And very aromatic.'

And very aromatic.'

central_sentences.sentence: But it will be hard for new foods to grow so popular with farmers and consumers that they become new staple crops, he thinks.

central_sentences.sentence: But Kudla didn't want to reveal which species were in his team's sights, because CRISPR has made the process so easy. 'Any one with the right skills could go to their lab and do this.'

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C. Joyce Van Eck。题干中的“Present efforts to domesticate one wild fruit are limited by the costs involved”可以通过以下几个线索词来定位：1. “Present efforts” 对应原文中的 “taking the work further”；2. “domesticate one wild fruit” 对应原文中的 “domesticate the groundcherry”；3. “limited by the costs involved” 对应原文中的 “would be expensive”。通过这些线索词，我们可以在原文中找到对应的句子，确认答案。

central_sentences.sentence: But she adds that taking the work further would be expensive because of the need to pay for a licence for the CRISPR technology and get regulatory approval.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A (Jorg Kudla)，因为他在文章中明确提到人类只利用了地球上很小比例的可食用植物。题干中的“small proportion”和“plant food available on Earth”在原文中分别对应“90 percent of our energy comes from just 15 crops”和“50,000 edible plants in the world”。这些表达清楚地表明了人类只利用了少量的植物资源。

groups.desc:
Questions 24–26

Complete the sentences below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect:
An undesirable trait such as loss of $224$may be caused by a mutation in a tomato gene.
By modifying one gene in a tomato plant, researchers made the tomato three times its original $225$.
A type of tomato which was not badly affected by$226$ , and was rich in vitamin C, was produced by a team of researchers in China.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "undesirable trait" 和 "loss of" 分别对应文章中的 "less desirable traits" 和 "lost much of"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认答案在 "lost much of their flavour" 这句话中。因此，答案是 "flavour"。这个答案是正确的，因为文章明确指出，超市种植的番茄品种失去了很多风味，这是一种不良特征。

central_sentences.sentence: For instance, the tomato strains grown for supermarkets have lost much of their flavour.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，为超市种植的番茄品种已经失去了大部分的风味。

target_sen.target_sen: And sometimes the desirable mutations come with less desirable traits.
For instance, the tomato strains grown for supermarkets have lost much of their flavour.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "modifying one gene" 对应文章中的 "editing a gene"，"three times its original" 对应文章中的 "tripled"。通过这些同义表达的对应关系，可以确定答案所在的句子。文章中明确提到 "they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT"，因此答案是 "size"。这个答案是正确的，因为题干问的是通过修改一个基因使番茄的大小变为原来的三倍，而文章中正好提到通过编辑基因使果实的大小增加了三倍。

central_sentences.sentence: For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，他们通过编辑一个名为FRUIT WEIGHT的基因使水果的大小增加了三倍，并通过编辑另一个名为MULTIFLORA的基因增加了每簇番茄的数量。

target_sen.target_sen: For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.
For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.
For instance, they tripled the size of fruit by editing a gene called FRUIT WEIGHT, and increased the number of tomatoes per truss by editing another called MULTIFLORA.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "salt"。题干中的 "not badly affected by" 对应文章中的 "tolerant"，而 "rich in vitamin C" 对应 "higher levels of vitamin C"。文章中明确提到中国团队创造了一种 "more salt tolerant" 的番茄品种，并且维生素C含量更高。因此，答案是 "salt"。

central_sentences.sentence: They also created another strain that is more salt tolerant – and has higher levels of vitamin C.
central_sentences.translation: 他们还创造了另一个品种，这个品种更耐盐，并且具有更高水平的维生素C。

target_sen.target_sen: They also created another strain that is more salt tolerant – and has higher levels of vitamin C.
They also created another strain that is more salt tolerant – and has higher levels of vitamin C.
The team in China re-domesticated several strains of wild tomatoes with desirable traits lost in domesticated tomatoes.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Scientific discovery is popularly believed to result from the sheer genius of such intellectual stars as naturalist Charles Darwin and theoretical physicist Albert Einstein. Our view of such unique contributions to science often disregards the person's prior experience and the efforts of their lesser-known predecessors. Conventional wisdom also places great weight on insight in promoting breakthrough scientific achievements, as if ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head – fully formed and functional.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: There may be some limited truth to this view. However, we believe that it largely misrepresents the real nature of scientific discovery, as well as that of creativity and innovation in many other realms of human endeavor.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Setting aside such greats as Darwin and Einstein – whose monumental contributions are duly celebrated – we suggest that innovation is more a process of trial and error, where two steps forward may sometimes come with one step back, as well as one or more steps to the right or left. This evolutionary view of human innovation undermines the notion of creative genius and recognizes the cumulative nature of scientific progress.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Consider one unheralded scientist: John Nicholson, a mathematical physicist working in the 1910s who postulated the existence of 'proto-elements' in outer space. By combining different numbers of weights of these proto-elements' atoms, Nicholson could recover the weights of all the elements in the then-known periodic table. These successes are all the more noteworthy given the fact that Nicholson was wrong about the presence of proto-elements: they do not actually exist. Yet, amid his often fanciful theories and wild speculations, Nicholson also proposed a novel theory about the structure of atoms. Niels Bohr, the Nobel prize-winning father of modern atomic theory, jumped off from this interesting idea to conceive his now-famous model of the atom.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: What are we to make of this story? One might simply conclude that science is a collective and cumulative enterprise. That may be true, but there may be a deeper insight to be gleaned. We propose that science is constantly evolving, much as species of animals do. In biological systems, organisms may display new characteristics that result from random genetic mutations. In the same way, random, arbitrary or accidental mutations of ideas may help pave the way for advances in science. If mutations prove beneficial, then the animal or the scientific theory will continue to thrive and perhaps reproduce.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Support for this evolutionary view of behavioral innovation comes from many domains. Consider one example of an influential innovation in US horseracing. The so-called 'acey-deucy' stirrup placement, in which the rider's foot in his left stirrup is placed as much as 25 centimeters lower than the right, is believed to confer important speed advantages when turning on oval tracks. It was developed by a relatively unknown jockey named Jackie Westrope. Had Westrope conducted methodical investigations or examined extensive film records in a shrewd plan to outrun his rivals? Had he foreseen the speed advantage that would be conferred by riding acey-deucy? No. He suffered a leg injury, which left him unable to fully bend his left knee. His modification just happened to coincide with enhanced left-hand turning performance. This led to the rapid and widespread adoption of riding acey-deucy by many riders, a racing style which continues in today's thoroughbred racing.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Plenty of other stories show that fresh advances can arise from error, misadventure, and also pure serendipity – a happy accident. For example, in the early 1970s, two employees of the company 3M each had a problem: Spencer Silver had a product – a glue which was only slightly sticky – and no use for it, while his colleague Art Fry was trying to figure out how to affix temporary bookmarks in his hymn book without damaging its pages. The solution to both these problems was the invention of the brilliantly simple yet phenomenally successful Post-It note. Such examples give lie to the claim that ingenious, designing minds are responsible for human creativity and invention. Far more banal and mechanical forces may be at work; forces that are fundamentally connected to the laws of science.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The notions of insight, creativity and genius are often invoked, but they remain vague and of doubtful scientific utility, especially when one considers the diverse and enduring contributions of individuals such as Plato, Leonardo da Vinci, Shakespeare, Beethoven, Galileo, Newton, Kepler, Curie, Pasteur and Edison. These notions merely label rather than explain the evolution of human innovations. We need another approach, and there is a promising candidate.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The Law of Effect was advanced by psychologist Edward Thorndike in 1898, some 40 years after Charles Darwin published his groundbreaking work on biological evolution, On the Origin of Species. This simple law holds that organisms tend to repeat successful behaviors and to refrain from performing unsuccessful ones. Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Of course, the origin of human innovation demands much further study. In particular, the provenance of the raw material on which the Law of Effect operates is not as clearly known as that of the genetic mutations on which the Law of Natural Selection operates. The generation of novel ideas and behaviors may not be entirely random, but constrained by prior successes and failures – of the current individual (such as Bohr) or of predecessors (such as Nicholson).

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: The time seems right for abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius, and for scientifically exploring the true origins of creative behavior.

groups.desc:
Questions 27–31

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: The purpose of the first paragraph is to
questions.analysis: 文章的第一段在概述一种普遍的假设，即科学发现被认为是由天才的灵感所驱动的。选项D中的“outline a common assumption”与文章中的“popularly believed”和“conventional wisdom”相对应，说明文章在描述一种普遍的看法。因此，正确答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: Scientific discovery is popularly believed to result from the sheer genius of such intellectual stars as naturalist Charles Darwin and theoretical physicist Albert Einstein.
central_sentences.translation: 人们普遍认为，科学发现源于像自然学家查尔斯·达尔文和理论物理学家阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦这样的知识巨星的纯粹天才。

target_sen.target_sen: Scientific discovery is popularly believed to result from the sheer genius of such intellectual stars as naturalist Charles Darwin and theoretical physicist Albert Einstein.

questions.title: What are the writers doing in the second paragraph?
questions.analysis: 在这句话中，作者提到“this view”并且使用了“misrepresents”这个词，表示对这个观点的批评。通过这些词汇可以看出，作者认为这个观点是错误的，并且误导了科学发现和创造力的真实本质。因此，正确答案是A：criticising an opinion。

central_sentences.sentence: However, we believe that it largely misrepresents the real nature of scientific discovery, as well as that of creativity and innovation in many other realms of human endeavor.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，我们认为这严重误导了科学发现的真实本质，以及人类在许多其他领域的创造力和创新的本质。

questions.title: In the third paragraph, what do the writers suggest about Darwin and Einstein?

options.text: They represent an exception to a general rule.
Their way of working has been misunderstood.
They are an ideal which others should aspire to.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到“Setting aside such greats as Darwin and Einstein”，这表明达尔文和爱因斯坦是一个例外，他们的工作方式与一般的创新过程不同。选项A的“represent an exception to a general rule”与此对应，因此是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Setting aside such greats as Darwin and Einstein – whose monumental contributions are duly celebrated – we suggest that innovation is more a process of trial and error, where two steps forward may sometimes come with one step back, as well as one or more steps to the right or left.
central_sentences.translation: 撇开达尔文和爱因斯坦这样伟大的人物不谈——他们的巨大贡献是应当被庆祝的——我们认为创新更多的是一个试错的过程，有时前进两步可能会伴随着后退一步，以及向右或向左移动一步或多步。

target_sen.target_sen: Setting aside such greats as Darwin and Einstein – whose monumental contributions are duly celebrated – we suggest that innovation is more a process of trial and error, where two steps forward may sometimes come with one step back, as well as one or more steps to the right or left.

syn_ary.synonym: such greats as Darwin and Einstein

target_sen.target_sen: Setting aside such greats as Darwin and Einstein – whose monumental contributions are duly celebrated – we suggest that innovation is more a process of trial and error, where two steps forward may sometimes come with one step back, as well as one or more steps to the right or left.

questions.title: John Nicholson is an example of a person whose idea

options.text: established his reputation as an influential scientist.
was only fully understood at a later point in history.
laid the foundations for someone else's breakthrough.
initially met with scepticism from the scientific community.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到，John Nicholson 提出了一个关于原子结构的新理论，Niels Bohr 从这个理论出发，构思了他著名的原子模型。这表明 Nicholson 的理论为 Bohr 的突破奠定了基础，因此选项 C 是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Nicholson also proposed a novel theory about the structure of atoms. Niels Bohr, the Nobel prize-winning father of modern atomic theory, jumped off from this interesting idea to conceive his now-famous model of the atom.
central_sentences.translation: 尼克尔森还提出了一个关于原子结构的新理论。现代原子理论之父、诺贝尔奖得主尼尔斯·玻尔从这个有趣的想法出发，构思了他现在著名的原子模型。

target_sen.target_sen: Consider one unheralded scientist: John Nicholson, a mathematical physicist working in the 1910s who postulated the existence of 'proto-elements' in outer space.
Yet, amid his often fanciful theories and wild speculations, Nicholson also proposed a novel theory about the structure of atoms.
Niels Bohr, the Nobel prize-winning father of modern atomic theory, jumped off from this interesting idea to conceive his now-famous model of the atom.
Niels Bohr, the Nobel prize-winning father of modern atomic theory, jumped off from this interesting idea to conceive his now-famous model of the atom.

questions.title: What is the key point of interest about the 'acey-deucy' stirrup placement?

options.text: the simple reason why it was invented
the enthusiasm with which it was adopted
the research that went into its development
the cleverness of the person who first used it

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 A，因为文章明确指出 Westrope 的腿部受伤导致了这种骑法的出现，这是一个简单且直接的原因。题干问的是关于“acey-deucy”马镫位置的关键点，而文章中解释了这种骑法的出现是因为 Westrope 的腿部受伤，这与选项 A 的“simple reason”相符。

central_sentences.sentence: He suffered a leg injury, which left him unable to fully bend his left knee. His modification just happened to coincide with enhanced left-hand turning performance.
central_sentences.translation: 他受了腿伤，导致无法完全弯曲左膝。他的这种改变恰好与提高左转弯性能相吻合。

target_sen.target_sen: His modification just happened to coincide with enhanced left-hand turning performance.

The so-called 'acey-deucy' stirrup placement, in which the rider's foot in his left stirrup is placed as much as 25 centimeters lower than the right, is believed to confer important speed advantages when turning on oval tracks.
Had he foreseen the speed advantage that would be conferred by riding acey-deucy?
This led to the rapid and widespread adoption of riding acey-deucy by many riders, a racing style which continues in today's thoroughbred racing.

groups.desc:
Questions 32–36

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Acknowledging people such as Plato or da Vinci as geniuses will help us understand the process by which great minds create new ideas.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“承认像柏拉图或达芬奇这样的人为天才将有助于我们理解伟大思想创造新想法的过程”。然而，文章明确表示，洞察力、创造力和天才的概念只是标签，并不能真正解释人类创新的演变。因此，题干的陈述与文章的观点相反，所以答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: These notions merely label rather than explain the evolution of human innovations.
central_sentences.translation: 这些概念仅仅是标签，而不能解释人类创新的演变。

target_sen.target_sen: The notions of insight, creativity and genius are often invoked, but they remain vague and of doubtful scientific utility, especially when one considers the diverse and enduring contributions of individuals such as Plato, Leonardo da Vinci, Shakespeare, Beethoven, Galileo, Newton, Kepler, Curie, Pasteur and Edison.
These notions merely label rather than explain the evolution of human innovations.
These notions merely label rather than explain the evolution of human innovations.

questions.title: The Law of Effect was discovered at a time when psychologists were seeking a scientific reason why creativity occurs.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“心理学家在寻找创造力产生的科学原因”在原文中并未提及。原文只提到效果律是由Edward Thorndike在1898年提出的，但没有说明当时心理学家是否在寻找创造力的科学原因。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The Law of Effect was advanced by psychologist Edward Thorndike in 1898, some 40 years after Charles Darwin published his groundbreaking work on biological evolution, On the Origin of Species.
central_sentences.translation: 效果律是由心理学家爱德华·桑代克在1898年提出的，大约在查尔斯·达尔文发表其开创性的生物进化著作《物种起源》40年后。

target_sen.target_sen: The Law of Effect was advanced by psychologist Edward Thorndike in 1898, some 40 years after Charles Darwin published his groundbreaking work on biological evolution, On the Origin of Species.
Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.
The Law of Effect was advanced by psychologist Edward Thorndike in 1898, some 40 years after Charles Darwin published his groundbreaking work on biological evolution, On the Origin of Species.

questions.title: The Law of Effect states that no planning is involved in the behaviour of organisms.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "no planning is involved" 对应文章中的 "without any end objective in sight"，而 "the behaviour of organisms" 对应文章中的 "organisms tend to repeat successful behaviors"。这些对应关系表明，效果法则确实描述了一个没有计划的过程，因此答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: This simple law holds that organisms tend to repeat successful behaviors and to refrain from performing unsuccessful ones. Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.
central_sentences.translation: 这个简单的法则认为，生物倾向于重复成功的行为，并避免执行不成功的行为。就像达尔文的自然选择法则一样，效果法则涉及一个完全机械的变异和选择过程，没有任何可见的最终目标。

target_sen.target_sen: The Law of Effect was advanced by psychologist Edward Thorndike in 1898, some 40 years after Charles Darwin published his groundbreaking work on biological evolution, On the Origin of Species.
Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.

syn_ary.synonym: without any end objective in sight

target_sen.target_sen: Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.

syn_ary.synonym: organisms tend to repeat successful behaviors

target_sen.target_sen: This simple law holds that organisms tend to repeat successful behaviors and to refrain from performing unsuccessful ones.

questions.title: The Law of Effect sets out clear explanations about the sources of new ideas and behaviours.
questions.analysis: 题干中提到 "The Law of Effect sets out clear explanations about the sources of new ideas and behaviours."，而原文中明确指出 Law of Effect 对新想法和行为的来源解释不清楚，使用了 "not as clearly known" 这个表达。因此，题干中的陈述与原文信息相反，所以正确答案是 NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Of course, the origin of human innovation demands much further study. In particular, the provenance of the raw material on which the Law of Effect operates is not as clearly known as that of the genetic mutations on which the Law of Natural Selection operates.
central_sentences.translation: 当然，人类创新的起源需要进一步研究。特别是，效果法则所作用的原材料的来源并不像自然选择法则所作用的基因突变那样清楚。

target_sen.target_sen: In particular, the provenance of the raw material on which the Law of Effect operates is not as clearly known as that of the genetic mutations on which the Law of Natural Selection operates.
In particular, the provenance of the raw material on which the Law of Effect operates is not as clearly known as that of the genetic mutations on which the Law of Natural Selection operates.
In particular, the provenance of the raw material on which the Law of Effect operates is not as clearly known as that of the genetic mutations on which the Law of Natural Selection operates.

locating_words.locating_word: sources of new ideas and behaviours

questions.title: Many scientists are now turning away from the notion of intelligent design and genius.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“Many scientists”在文章中没有明确提到。虽然文章提到了“abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius”，但没有提到“Many scientists”正在这样做。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到完全对应的内容，答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The time seems right for abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius, and for scientifically exploring the true origins of creative behavior.
central_sentences.translation: 现在似乎是时候放弃关于智能设计和天才的天真观念，并科学地探索创造性行为的真正起源了。

target_sen.target_sen: The time seems right for abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius, and for scientifically exploring the true origins of creative behavior.
The time seems right for abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius, and for scientifically exploring the true origins of creative behavior.
The time seems right for abandoning the naive notions of intelligent design and genius, and for scientifically exploring the true origins of creative behavior.

groups.title: The origins of creative behaviour
groups.desc:
Questions 37–40

Complete the summary using the list of words, A–G, below.
groups.collect: The traditional view of scientific discovery is that breakthroughs happen when a single great mind has sudden $237$ . Although this can occur, it is not often the case. Advances are more likely to be the result of a longer process. In some cases, this process involves $238$ , such as Nicholson's theory about proto-elements. In others, simple necessity may provoke innovation, as with Westrope's decision to modify the position of his riding stirrups. There is also often an element of $239$ , for example, the coincidence of ideas that led to the invention of the Post-It note. With both the Law of Natural Selection and the Law of Effect, there may be no clear $240$ involved, but merely a process of variation and selection.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "sudden" 对应原文中的 "spontaneously"，"great mind" 对应 "intellectual stars" 和 "unique contributions"，"breakthrough" 对应 "breakthrough scientific achievements"。这些关键词在原文中都能找到对应的同义替换词。原文提到 "ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head"，这与 "sudden inspiration" 的意思最为接近，因此正确答案是 "inspiration"。

central_sentences.sentence: Conventional wisdom also places great weight on insight in promoting breakthrough scientific achievements, as if ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head – fully formed and functional.
central_sentences.translation: 传统观点也非常重视洞察力在促进科学突破性成就中的作用，就好像想法会自发地在某人的头脑中产生 - 完全成型且可以立即使用。

target_sen.target_sen: Conventional wisdom also places great weight on insight in promoting breakthrough scientific achievements, as if ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head – fully formed and functional.
Conventional wisdom also places great weight on insight in promoting breakthrough scientific achievements, as if ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head – fully formed and functional.
Conventional wisdom also places great weight on insight in promoting breakthrough scientific achievements, as if ideas spontaneously pop into someone's head – fully formed and functional.

questions.analysis: 题干中提到 "Nicholson's theory about proto-elements"，而原文中明确指出 "Nicholson was wrong about the presence of proto-elements"，这表明 Nicholson 的理论是错误的。因此，正确答案是 "mistakes"。这道题考查学生对文章细节的理解，特别是对错误在科学发现中的作用的理解。

central_sentences.sentence: These successes are all the more noteworthy given the fact that Nicholson was wrong about the presence of proto-elements: they do not actually exist.
central_sentences.translation: 考虑到尼科尔森关于原始元素存在的理论是错误的这一事实，这些成功更加值得注意：这些原始元素实际上并不存在。

syn_ary.synonym: Nicholson was wrong about the presence of proto-elements

target_sen.target_sen: These successes are all the more noteworthy given the fact that Nicholson was wrong about the presence of proto-elements: they do not actually exist.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "coincidence" 对应原文中的 "serendipity" 和 "happy accident"，这两个词都表示偶然的巧合或幸运的意外。因此，正确答案是 "luck"。

central_sentences.sentence: Plenty of other stories show that fresh advances can arise from error, misadventure, and also pure serendipity – a happy accident.
central_sentences.translation: 许多其他的故事表明，新的进展可能源于错误、意外，以及纯粹的机缘巧合——一个幸运的意外。

target_sen.target_sen: Plenty of other stories show that fresh advances can arise from error, misadventure, and also pure serendipity – a happy accident.
Plenty of other stories show that fresh advances can arise from error, misadventure, and also pure serendipity – a happy accident.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "no clear" 和 "involved" 在原文中对应的是 "without any" 和 "in sight"。原文提到 "without any end objective in sight"，这与题干中的 "no clear 【 】 involved" 是同义表达。因此，正确答案是 "goals"。

central_sentences.sentence: Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.
central_sentences.translation: 就像达尔文的自然选择定律一样，效果定律涉及一个完全机械的变异和选择过程，没有任何明确的最终目标。

target_sen.target_sen: Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.
Just like Darwin's Law of Natural Selection, the Law of Effect involves an entirely mechanical process of variation and selection, without any end objective in sight.
